email.subject: Weekly product report — April 1–7
email.body: Product metrics for the week of April 1–7: Active users 1,247 (+8% WoW), Agent sessions 4,820, Avg session quality score 7.4/10, Top feature used: inbox triage. Full report in dashboard.

email.subject: Travel booked — Paris April 2 (updated: April 3 return)
email.body: Hi Andriy, updated travel confirmed: Eurostar departs April 2 at 7:08am, returns April 3 at 6:30pm. Hotel: Le Marais boutique hotel, room confirmed. Conference check-in 1pm April 2. Your keynote is at 3pm. Dinner with Fatima Al-Rashid is NOT confirmed — she may approach you informally at the conference.

email.subject: Re: Term sheet — initial comments
email.body: Andriy, I've reviewed the first term sheet draft. Broadly workable. The two issues I flagged on our call (liquidation preference, anti-dilution) are the only sticking points. I'd suggest a call with our lawyers next week to get these resolved. I'm confident we can close this by April 18.

email.subject: Team pulse survey results — Q1
email.body: Hi Andriy, Q1 team pulse results are in. Overall score: 7.8/10 (up from 7.2 in Q4). Highlights: clarity of direction improved significantly. Areas to watch: work-life balance score dropped slightly (6.9). I've put the full breakdown in the People doc. Worth 10 minutes in your next team all-hands.

email.subject: Re: Technical specs — received, reviewing
email.body: Hi, thanks for sending the API spec and agent integration docs. Our team is reviewing this week. Initial read looks promising — the context retrieval architecture is exactly what we need. I'll come back with specific questions by April 11. One early flag: we'll need the dedicated SLA confirmed in the contract, not just in the docs.

email.subject: Inbox classifier retrain — results
email.body: Hi Andriy, the inbox classifier retrain is complete. Purpose classification accuracy: 91.4% (up from 87.2%). Notably improved: escalation vs. inform distinction and multi-intent messages. Confidence calibration is better too — the model is now more honest about borderline cases (lower scores on ambiguous emails). Deploying to staging today.

email.subject: Sales call — Aeroform discovery notes
email.body: Hi Andriy, just off the Aeroform discovery call with Marc Dubois. Really positive signal — he's clearly the decision-maker and is genuinely interested. His main concerns: EU data residency and implementation timeline. I think we have a strong shot at this. I'll draft the proposal this week and share before sending. Key ask from Marc: a reference customer in manufacturing if we have one.

email.subject: April kick-off — key dates and priorities
email.body: Hi Andriy, April summary: Key dates: April 2 Paris conference, April 22 La Product Conf, April 24 DataPulse demo, April 28 Board meeting. Priorities: Board prep + Nexus renewal + Series B close. Your calendar is tight — I've blocked Monday mornings for focused work. Travel for Paris confirmed. Let me know if anything needs adjusting.

email.subject: Portfolio update — Q1 review
email.body: Hi Andriy, following our Q1 portfolio review, I wanted to share some benchmarks from comparable companies in our portfolio. The unit economics slide in your deck would be stronger with these comparables. Happy to share the data if useful for the Series B materials.

email.subject: Board deck — first draft structure
email.body: Hi Andriy, the board deck skeleton is ready in the shared drive. Structure follows our usual format: market context, Q1 performance, Q2 roadmap, financial projections, team update, and ask. I've left placeholders for slides you'll want to populate personally (unit economics, Series B update). Sophie's comments from last time are also noted. Target: complete draft by April 14.

email.subject: Senior ML Engineer — job spec approved, posting today
email.body: Hi Andriy, the job spec for Senior ML Engineer has been approved and is going live today. We're targeting 4–6 weeks to shortlist. Key channels: LinkedIn, tech-specific job boards, and referrals. I'll send weekly updates. Any specific referrals from your network would be helpful at this stage.

email.subject: Agent pipeline v2 — architecture approved
email.body: Hi Andriy, the architecture review went well yesterday. The team is aligned on decoupling context retrieval from action execution. This approach will make the data contract enforcement points much cleaner. Starting implementation next sprint. ETA for v2 alpha: May 15.

email.subject: Re: Renewal kick-off — call notes
email.body: Hi Andriy, great call today. Confirming the key points: expanded usage will add approximately 40% to current volume, you'll include the new agent integration features at no uplift for year 1, and pricing will be adjusted at year 2. I'll send a summary note to my team. Can your team send the updated technical specs and NDA by April 1?

email.subject: Q1 retrospective — summary
email.body: Hi Andriy, attaching the Q1 retrospective summary. Went well — the team is proud of shipping the agent context API on time. Main lessons: we underestimated the data contract complexity for the calendar sync, and the hiring pipeline was slower than planned. These are the two focus areas we want to address in Q2 planning.

email.subject: Notion: 'Agentic Contract Spec' page updated by Omar Hassan
email.body: Omar Hassan made 12 edits to 'Agentic Contract Spec'. View changes in Notion.

email.subject: Q1 sales close — final numbers
email.body: Hi Andriy, final Q1 numbers: €2.1M closed (vs €1.9M target). Key wins: 2 new enterprise clients, Nexus renewal pre-negotiation started. Pipeline entering Q2: €6M total, €4.2M qualified. The Aeroform and DataPulse opportunities were not in the Q1 plan — both are upside for Q2.

email.subject: Speaking confirmation — La Product Conf, April 22
email.body: Hi Andriy, La Product Conf has confirmed your speaking slot: April 22, 2:30pm, main stage. Title: 'Building Data Products for Agent Consumers'. 25 minutes + 5 minutes Q&A. I've blocked travel and prep time. They'll want the abstract and bio by March 31. I can draft if you want.

email.subject: Contacts dataset — provenance audit complete
email.body: Hi Andriy, completed the provenance audit on the contacts dataset. Results: 58% human_entered, 31% crm_sync, 11% inferred. The inferred records are mostly cold inbounds and conference connections. I recommend we add a last_verified_at field and set a 30-day review flag for any inferred record that hasn't been touched. This will help the agent treat these contacts more conservatively.

email.subject: NDA for Nexus — received and starting review
email.body: Andriy, I've received the NDA documents from Nexus. Initial scan — it's a fairly standard commercial NDA with some IP clauses that will need attention. I'll have preliminary comments by March 28. If there are specific clauses you want me to prioritise, let me know.

email.subject: Board governance agenda — AI systems risk
email.body: Andriy, as discussed informally at the last board dinner, I'd like to add AI systems governance to the April board agenda. Specifically: how are we thinking about agent autonomy limits, what oversight mechanisms are in place, and how do we report on AI risk to shareholders. This is becoming a governance expectation for companies at our stage. Can we plan 20 minutes for this topic?
